access.subtitle: This account uses the system according to administrator policies
access.platform_admin_description: This account is for SaaS console operations. It can manage tenants, SaaS models, and SaaS-level semantic configuration, but does not enter tenant-side ChatBI workspaces.
access.collab_admin_description: This account can maintain reusable collaboration settings and Agents across organizations. Business data still follows the current organization and project permissions.
access.normal_user_description: This account has not joined any organization. It can request a new organization or join someone else's organization before entering tenant-side workspaces.
access.tenant_owner_description: This account owns member access, security policies, data requests, and owner-level operations for the current organization.
access.tenant_admin_description: This account can manage members, projects, semantic layer records, and permission policies inside the current organization.
access.tenant_member_description: This account can ask questions, use dashboards, and use assistants within authorized projects. More data or management access requires an administrator.
access.full_description: This account has full system management access for SaaS settings, projects, and permission policies.
access.limited_management_description: This account has limited management access. Data scope and features still follow administrator policies.
access.limited_description: This account has limited access and can use features and data scopes opened by administrators.
access.qa_description: Ask data questions within the authorized scope. Results are governed by administrator policies.
access.dashboard_description: Create and view dashboards based on opened data capabilities.
access.analysis_assistant_description: Use the assistant for analysis summaries. Analysis still follows permission policies.
access.data_scope_description: Only an overview is shown. Specific projects, tables, fields, or filter rules are not displayed.
access.no_tenant_data_scope_description: No organization has been joined yet, so there are no organization projects or data scopes.
access.admin_settings_description: System settings and permission policies are maintained by administrators.
access.privacy_description: To avoid exposing data boundaries, specific projects, tables, fields, row rules, or restricted lists are not shown here.
access.apply_tip: To expand data scope or enable more capabilities, please contact an administrator.
access.prompt_not_visible: This Agent prompt is maintained by administrators and is not visible to this account.
access.workspace_identity_description: This page shows the workspaces you have joined and your role in each one. Specific data sources, tables, fields, and row rules are still controlled by administrator policies.
access.workspace_role_groups_description: Only the roles you currently hold and their responsibilities are shown here. Use the identity list above as the source for joined workspaces.
access.pending_workspace_invitations_descriptio: Workspace owners or admins invited you to join. Accept or reject the invitations here.
access.pending_workspace_requests_description: Your submitted workspace requests and invitations waiting for your response are shown here.
access.owner_group_description: You carry final responsibility for these workspaces and can decide member access, security, and key data policies.
access.owner_capability_members: Manage member access, member roles, and admin appointments.
access.owner_capability_security: Maintain workspace security policies, data requests, and key settings.
access.owner_capability_data: Oversee how data sources, semantic records, assistants, and dashboards are opened.
access.owner_responsibility_boundary: Keep workspace data boundaries and authorization rules clear and reliable.
access.owner_responsibility_trust: Be accountable for member data use, shared assets, and new member invitations.
access.owner_responsibility_risk: Handle high-risk changes, exits, transfers, and abnormal access issues.
access.admin_group_description: You manage daily operations for these workspaces, including members, project settings, semantic assets, and permission policies.
access.admin_capability_members: Handle invitations, access reviews, and member role changes.
access.admin_capability_config: Configure data sources, permission policies, semantic records, Data Skills, and Agents.
access.admin_capability_support: Support member Q&A, dashboard building, and assistant usage.
access.admin_responsibility_policy: Maintain permissions according to workspace rules without expanding data scope without a basis.
access.admin_responsibility_support: Help members understand available data, definitions, and asset usage.
access.admin_responsibility_data: Fill semantic, training SQL, or permission gaps when configuration is incomplete.
access.member_group_title: Workspaces Where I Am a Member
access.member_group_description: You mainly use opened data capabilities in these workspaces: ask questions, view dashboards, and use assistants.
access.member_capability_analysis: Ask questions and analyze data inside authorized projects.
access.member_capability_assets: View or use opened dashboards, Data Skills, and custom Agents.
access.member_capability_request: Request more data or capabilities from an administrator when needed.
access.member_responsibility_scope: Use data only within the authorized scope and do not bypass project or data permissions.
access.member_responsibility_share: Consider data sensitivity and context when sharing analysis conclusions.
access.member_responsibility_request: Clearly describe missing definitions, fields, or project permissions when requesting help.
access.permission_boundary_description: This page explains workspace-level identity and responsibility. It does not expose specific data sources, tables, fields, row rules, or restricted lists. Q&A, dashboard, and assistant results still follow the current workspace and project permissions.
